args.code:
import json
import re

# Load the Europe metadata
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Load some sample articles to understand the data
articles_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(articles_file, 'r') as f:
    sample_articles = json.load(f)

# Create a dictionary of article_id to article data for sample
sample_dict = {article['article_id']: article for article in sample_articles}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'europe_articles_count': len(europe_metadata),
    'sample_articles_count': len(sample_articles),
    'sample_article': sample_articles[0] if sample_articles else None
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load Europe metadata

with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

europe_articles_per_year = {}
for article in europe_metadata:

    europe_articles_per_year[year] = europe_articles_per_year.get(year, 0) + 1

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(europe_articles_per_year))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Create mapping of article_id to year
europe_by_article_id = {}
for article in europe_metadata:
    article_id = article['article_id']
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    europe_by_article_id[article_id] = year

# Get all unique article IDs from Europe
all_europe_ids = list(europe_by_article_id.keys())
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_europe_ids': len(all_europe_ids),
    'sample_ids': all_europe_ids[:10]
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load the full Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Create dictionaries for fast lookup
article_id_to_year = {}
year_to_article_ids = {}

for article in europe_metadata:
    article_id = article['article_id']
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    article_id_to_year[article_id] = year
    
    if year not in year_to_article_ids:
        year_to_article_ids[year] = []
    year_to_article_ids[year].append(article_id)

# Check which years we have
years = sorted([int(y) for y in year_to_article_ids.keys() if 2010 <= int(y) <= 2020])
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_europe_articles': len(europe_metadata),
    'article_ids_sample': list(article_id_to_year.keys())[:10],
    'years_in_range': years,
    'articles_per_year': {year: len(year_to_article_ids[str(year)]) for year in years}
}))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Sample business article analysis
sample_business_articles = [
    {"article_id": "0", "title": "Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again."},
    {"article_id": "1", "title": "Carlyle Looks Toward Commercial Aerospace (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Private investment firm Carlyle Group..."},
    {"article_id": "2", "title": "Oil and Economy Cloud Stocks' Outlook (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Soaring crude prices plus worries\about the economy..."},
    {"article_id": "13", "title": "Google IPO Auction Off to Rocky Start", "description": "WASHINGTON/NEW YORK (Reuters) - The auction for Google Inc.'s highly anticipated initial public offering..."},
    {"article_id": "14", "title": "Dollar Falls Broadly on Record Trade Gap", "description": "NEW YORK (Reuters) - The dollar tumbled broadly on Friday after data showing a record U.S. trade deficit..."},
    {"article_id": "20", "title": "Google IPO faces Playboy slip-up", "description": "The bidding gets underway for Google's public offering..."},
    {"article_id": "21", "title": "Eurozone economy keeps growing", "description": "Official figures show the 12-nation eurozone economy continues to grow..."}
]

# Business keywords - identified from samples
business_keywords = [
    'wall st', 'stock', 'stocks', 'ipo', 'dollar', 'economy', 'economic', 'trade', 'oil price', 'oil prices',
    'investment', 'investing', 'market', 'markets', 'finance', 'financial', 'bank', 'banking', 'profit',
    'business', 'commercial', 'company', 'companies', 'shares', 'share price', 'nasdaq', 'nyse', 's&p',
    'quarterly', 'earnings', 'revenue', 'sales', 'growth', 'rates', 'interest rates', 'bond', 'bonds',
    'hedge fund', 'hedge funds', 'capital', 'asset', 'assets', 'merger', 'acquisition', 'buyout'
]

def is_business_article(title, description):
    """Classify article as business based on keywords"""
    text = (title or '' + ' ' + description or '').lower()
    
    for keyword in business_keywords:
        if keyword in text:
            return True
    
    # Additional indicators
    if re.search(r'\$\d+|\d+\.\d+bn|\d+\.\d+billion', text):
        return True
        
    return False

# Test classifier on sample
results = []
for article in sample_business_articles:
    is_business = is_business_article(article['title'], article['description'])
    results.append({
        'article_id': article['article_id'],
        'title': article['title'],
        'is_business': is_business
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'classification_results': results,
    'business_keywords_count': len(business_keywords)
}))

import json
import re

business_keywords = [
    'wall st', 'stock', 'stocks', 'ipo', 'dollar', 'economy', 'economic', 'trade', 'oil price', 'oil prices',
    'investment', 'investing', 'market', 'markets', 'finance', 'financial', 'bank', 'banking', 'profit',
    'business', 'commercial', 'company', 'companies', 'shares', 'share price', 'nasdaq', 'nyse', 's&p',
    'quarterly', 'earnings', 'revenue', 'sales', 'growth', 'rates', 'interest rates', 'bond', 'bonds',
    'hedge fund', 'hedge funds', 'capital', 'asset', 'assets', 'merger', 'acquisition', 'buyout'
]

def is_business_article(title, description):
    text = (title or '' + ' ' + description or '').lower()
    for keyword in business_keywords:
        if keyword in text:
            return True
    return False

sample_articles = [
    {"article_id": "0", "title": "Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again."},
    {"article_id": "1", "title": "Carlyle Looks Toward Commercial Aerospace (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Private investment firm Carlyle Group..."},
    {"article_id": "2", "title": "Oil and Economy Cloud Stocks' Outlook (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Soaring crude prices plus worries\about the economy and the outlook for earnings."}
]

results = []
for article in sample_articles:
    is_business = is_business_article(article['title'], article['description'])
    results.append({'article_id': article['article_id'], 'is_business': is_business})

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(results))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Build article_id to year mapping for Europe articles 2010-2020
europe_article_years = {}
for article in europe_metadata:
    article_id = str(article['article_id'])  # String for matching with MongoDB
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    europe_article_years[article_id] = year

# Business keywords for classification
business_keywords = [
    'wall st', 'stock', 'stocks', 'ipo', 'dollar', 'economy', 'economic', 'trade',
    'oil price', 'oil prices', 'investment', 'investing', 'market', 'markets',
    'finance', 'financial', 'bank', 'banking', 'profit', 'business', 'commercial',
    'company', 'companies', 'shares', 'share price', 'nasdaq', 'nyse', 's&p',
    'quarterly', 'earnings', 'revenue', 'sales', 'growth', 'rates',
    'interest rates', 'bond', 'bonds', 'hedge fund', 'hedge funds',
    'capital', 'asset', 'assets', 'merger', 'acquisition', 'buyout',
    'bull market', 'bear market', 'wall street', 'federal reserve', 'fed'
]

def is_business_article(title, description):
    """Classify article as business based on keywords"""
    if not title and not description:
        return False
    
    text = ((title or '') + ' ' + (description or '')).lower()
    
    for keyword in business_keywords:
        if keyword in text:
            return True
    
    # Check for money patterns
    if '$' in text or re.search(r'\b\d+\.\d+bn\b', text) or re.search(r'\b\d+\.\d+billion\b', text):
        return True
        
    return False

# Batch size for processing
BATCH_SIZE = 500
all_article_ids = list(europe_article_years.keys())
total_articles = len(all_article_ids)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_europe_articles': total_articles,
    'years_range': '2010-2020',
    'batch_size': BATCH_SIZE,
    'num_batches': (total_articles + BATCH_SIZE - 1) // BATCH_SIZE
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Get all article IDs from Europe (2010-2020)
all_article_ids = [str(article['article_id']) for article in europe_metadata]

# Process in smaller batches
BATCH_SIZE = 1000  # Smaller batch
num_batches = (len(all_article_ids) + BATCH_SIZE - 1) // BATCH_SIZE

# Get first batch of article IDs
first_batch_ids = all_article_ids[:BATCH_SIZE]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(all_article_ids),
    'batch_size': BATCH_SIZE,
    'num_batches': num_batches,
    'first_batch_ids': first_batch_ids[:10]
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load Europe metadata and sample articles
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
sample_articles = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']

# Europe metadata is a file path, sample articles are direct data
deurope_file = europe_file
if isinstance(europe_file, str) and '.json' in europe_file:
    with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
        europe_metadata = json.load(f)
else:
    europe_metadata = europe_file

# Check data structure
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'europe_metadata_type': type(europe_metadata).__name__,
    'sample_articles_type': type(sample_articles).__name__,
    'europe_count': len(europe_metadata) if isinstance(europe_metadata, list) else 'not list',
    'sample_count': len(sample_articles) if isinstance(sample_articles, list) else 'not list'
}))

import json

# Load Europe metadata and sample articles
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
sample_articles = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']

# Europe metadata is a file path, sample articles are direct data
deurope_file = europe_file
if isinstance(europe_file, str) and '.json' in europe_file:
    with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
        europe_metadata = json.load(f)
else:
    europe_metadata = europe_file

# Check data structure
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'europe_metadata_type': type(europe_metadata).__name__,
    'sample_articles_type': type(sample_articles).__name__,
    'europe_count': len(europe_metadata) if isinstance(europe_metadata, list) else 'not list',
    'sample_count': len(sample_articles) if isinstance(sample_articles, list) else 'not list'
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Count articles by year for Europe
articles_by_year = {}
for article in europe_metadata:
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    articles_by_year[year] = articles_by_year.get(year, 0) + 1

# Show the data we have
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(articles_by_year))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Create mapping of article_id to year
article_to_year = {}
for article in europe_metadata:
    article_to_year[str(article['article_id'])] = article['publication_date'][:4]

# Get unique years
years = sorted(set(article_to_year.values()))
years_2010_2020 = [y for y in years if 2010 <= int(y) <= 2020]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'years_with_data': years,
    'years_in_range': years_2010_2020,
    'total_europe_articles': len(article_to_year),
    'articles_2010_2020': len([y for y in article_to_year.values() if 2010 <= int(y) <= 2020])
}))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Business keywords - expanded based on domain expertise
business_keywords = [
    # Financial markets
    'wall st', 'stock', 'stocks', 'ipo', 'dollar', 'share', 'shares', 'nasdaq', 'nyse', 's&p',
    'bull market', 'bear market', 'stock market', 'equity', 'equities', 'futures', 'options',
    # Companies and business
    'company', 'companies', 'corporation', 'corporate', 'firm', 'investment', 'investing',
    'business', 'commercial', 'enterprise', 'conglomerate', 'industry', 'industries',
    # Economics
    'economy', 'economic', 'trade', 'deficit', 'growth', 'recession', 'inflation', 'gdp',
    'economist', 'economists', 'economic data', 'economic growth',
    # Finance
    'finance', 'financial', 'bank', 'banking', 'profit', 'loss', 'revenue', 'earnings',
    'quarterly', 'annual', 'fiscal', 'capital', 'asset', 'assets', 'liability', 'bond', 'bonds',
    # Oil & commodities
    'oil price', 'oil prices', 'crude oil', 'commodity', 'commodities', 'energy price', 'energy prices',
    # M&A
    'merger', 'acquisition', 'buyout', 'takeover', 'bid', 'bidding', 'auction',
    # Monetary/Fiscal
    'federal reserve', 'the fed', 'interest rate', 'interest rates', 'rate cut', 'rate hike',
    'monetary policy', 'fiscal policy', 'treasury', 'treasuries',
    # Common phrases
    'public offering', 'quarterly results', 'trading', 'debt', 'credit', 'loan'
]

sports_keywords = [
    'game', 'games', 'sport', 'sports', 'olympics', 'world cup', 'championship',
    'team', 'teams', 'league', 'leagues', 'player', 'players', 'coach', 'coaches'
]

world_keywords = [
    'war', 'peace', 'conflict', 'diplomat', 'diplomacy', 'united nations', 'un',
    'treaty', 'treaties', 'government', 'governments', 'election', 'elections'
]

sci_tech_keywords = [
    'technology', 'tech', 'science', 'scientific', 'research', 'researcher', 'researchers',
    'innovation', 'digital', 'internet', 'ai', 'artificial intelligence', 'robot', 'robots'
]

def classify_article(title, description):
    """Classify article into one of the 4 categories"""
    if not title and not description:
        return 'World'  # Default fallback
    
    text = ((title or '') + ' ' + (description or '')).lower()
    
    # Check each category
    business_score = sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text)
    sports_score = sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text)
    world_score = sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text)
    sci_tech_score = sum(1 for kw in sci_tech_keywords if kw in text)
    
    # Find max score
    scores = {
        'Business': business_score,
        'Sports': sports_score,
        'World': world_score,
        'Science/Technology': sci_tech_score
    }
    
    # Additional money patterns boost business score
    if re.search(r'\$[\d,\.]+', text) or re.search(r'\b\d+\.\d+bn\b', text):
        scores['Business'] += 2
    
    # Return category with highest score
    return max(scores, key=scores.get)

# Test classifier on some samples
sample_articles = [
    {"article_id": "0", "title": "Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again."},
    {"article_id": "3", "title": "Iraq Halts Oil Exports", "description": "Reuters - Authorities have halted oil export"},
    {"article_id": "21", "title": "Eurozone economy keeps growing", "description": "Official figures show economy continues to grow"},
    {"article_id": "13", "title": "Google IPO Auction Off to Rocky Start", "description": "The auction for Google Inc.'s highly anticipated initial public offering"}
]

test_results = []
for article in sample_articles:
    category = classify_article(article['title'], article['description'])
    test_results.append({
        'article_id': article['article_id'],
        'title': article['title'],
        'category': category
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(test_results))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Business keywords - expanded based on domain expertise
business_keywords = [
    # Financial markets
    'wall st', 'stock', 'stocks', 'ipo', 'dollar', 'share', 'shares', 'nasdaq', 'nyse', 's&p',
    'bull market', 'bear market', 'stock market', 'equity', 'equities', 'futures', 'options',
    # Companies and business
    'company', 'companies', 'corporation', 'corporate', 'firm', 'investment', 'investing',
    'business', 'commercial', 'enterprise', 'conglomerate', 'industry', 'industries',
    # Economics
    'economy', 'economic', 'trade', 'deficit', 'growth', 'recession', 'inflation', 'gdp',
    'economist', 'economists', 'economic data', 'economic growth',
    # Finance
    'finance', 'financial', 'bank', 'banking', 'profit', 'loss', 'revenue', 'earnings',
    'quarterly', 'annual', 'fiscal', 'capital', 'asset', 'assets', 'liability', 'bond', 'bonds',
    # Oil & commodities
    'oil price', 'oil prices', 'crude oil', 'commodity', 'commodities', 'energy price', 'energy prices',
    # M&A
    'merger', 'acquisition', 'buyout', 'takeover', 'bid', 'bidding', 'auction',
    # Monetary/Fiscal
    'federal reserve', 'the fed', 'interest rate', 'interest rates', 'rate cut', 'rate hike',
    'monetary policy', 'fiscal policy', 'treasury', 'treasuries',
    # Common phrases
    'public offering', 'quarterly results', 'trading', 'debt', 'credit', 'loan'
]

sports_keywords = [
    'game', 'games', 'sport', 'sports', 'olympics', 'world cup', 'championship',
    'team', 'teams', 'league', 'leagues', 'player', 'players', 'coach', 'coaches'
]

world_keywords = [
    'war', 'peace', 'conflict', 'diplomat', 'diplomacy', 'united nations', 'un',
    'treaty', 'treaties', 'government', 'governments', 'election', 'elections'
]

sci_tech_keywords = [
    'technology', 'tech', 'science', 'scientific', 'research', 'researcher', 'researchers',
    'innovation', 'digital', 'internet', 'ai', 'artificial intelligence', 'robot', 'robots'
]

def classify_article(title, description):
    """Classify article into one of the 4 categories"""
    if not title and not description:
        return 'World'  # Default fallback
    
    text = ((title or '') + ' ' + (description or '')).lower()
    
    # Check each category
    business_score = sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text)
    sports_score = sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text)
    world_score = sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text)
    sci_tech_score = sum(1 for kw in sci_tech_keywords if kw in text)
    
    # Find max score
    scores = {
        'Business': business_score,
        'Sports': sports_score,
        'World': world_score,
        'Science/Technology': sci_tech_score
    }
    
    # Additional money patterns boost business score
    if re.search(r'\$[\d,\.]+', text) or re.search(r'\b\d+\.\d+bn\b', text):
        scores['Business'] += 2
    
    # Return category with highest score
    return max(scores, key=scores.get)

# Test classifier on some samples
sample_articles = [
    {"article_id": "0", "title": "Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again."},
    {"article_id": "3", "title": "Iraq Halts Oil Exports", "description": "Reuters - Authorities have halted oil export"},
    {"article_id": "21", "title": "Eurozone economy keeps growing", "description": "Official figures show economy continues to grow"},
    {"article_id": "13", "title": "Google IPO Auction Off to Rocky Start", "description": "The auction for Google Inc.'s highly anticipated initial public offering"}
]

test_results = []
for article in sample_articles:
    category = classify_article(article['title'], article['description'])
    test_results.append({
        'article_id': article['article_id'],
        'title': article['title'],
        'category': category
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(test_results))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Create article_id to year mapping
article_to_year = {}
for article in europe_metadata:
    article_id = str(article['article_id'])
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    article_to_year[article_id] = year

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'total_articles': len(article_to_year), 'sample_ids': list(article_to_year.keys())[:10]}))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Create article_id to year mapping
article_to_year = {}
for article in europe_metadata:
    article_id = str(article['article_id'])
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    article_to_year[article_id] = year

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'total_articles': len(article_to_year), 'sample_ids': list(article_to_year.keys())[:10]}))

args.code:
import json

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Calculate articles per year for Europe (already have the data from earlier calculation)
articles_per_year = {
    '2010': 1306, '2011': 1316, '2012': 1355, '2013': 1354,
    '2014': 1372, '2015': 1357, '2016': 1364, '2017': 1391,
    '2018': 1351, '2019': 1328, '2020': 1366
}

# Calculate average
years_count = len(articles_per_year)
total_articles = sum(articles_per_year.values())
average_per_year = total_articles / years_count if years_count > 0 else 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'articles_per_year': articles_per_year,
    'total_years': years_count,
    'total_articles': total_articles,
    'average_per_year': round(average_per_year, 2)
}))

val_args.code:
import json

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Calculate articles per year for Europe (already have the data from earlier calculation)
articles_per_year = {
    '2010': 1306, '2011': 1316, '2012': 1355, '2013': 1354,
    '2014': 1372, '2015': 1357, '2016': 1364, '2017': 1391,
    '2018': 1351, '2019': 1328, '2020': 1366
}

# Calculate average
years_count = len(articles_per_year)
total_articles = sum(articles_per_year.values())
average_per_year = total_articles / years_count if years_count > 0 else 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'articles_per_year': articles_per_year,
    'total_years': years_count,
    'total_articles': total_articles,
    'average_per_year': round(average_per_year, 2)
}))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Create year-to-article_ids mapping and get all article IDs
year_to_article_ids = {}
all_article_ids = []

for article in europe_metadata:
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    article_id = article['article_id']  # Keep as string to match MongoDB
    
    # Only include 2010-2020
    if 2010 <= int(year) <= 2020:
        all_article_ids.append(article_id)
        if year not in year_to_article_ids:
            year_to_article_ids[year] = []
        year_to_article_ids[year].append(article_id)

# Sort years
sorted_years = sorted(year_to_article_ids.keys())

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'years': sorted_years,
    'articles_per_year': {year: len(ids) for year, ids in year_to_article_ids.items()},
    'total_articles': len(all_article_ids),
    'sample_ids': all_article_ids[:10]
}))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Create year-to-article_ids mapping and get all article IDs
year_to_article_ids = {}
all_article_ids = []

for article in europe_metadata:
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    article_id = article['article_id']  # Keep as string to match MongoDB
    
    # Only include 2010-2020
    if 2010 <= int(year) <= 2020:
        all_article_ids.append(article_id)
        if year not in year_to_article_ids:
            year_to_article_ids[year] = []
        year_to_article_ids[year].append(article_id)

# Sort years
sorted_years = sorted(year_to_article_ids.keys())

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'years': sorted_years,
    'articles_per_year': {year: len(ids) for year, ids in year_to_article_ids.items()},
    'total_articles': len(all_article_ids),
    'sample_ids': all_article_ids[:10]
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load Europe metadata
europe_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
with open(europe_file, 'r') as f:
    europe_metadata = json.load(f)

# Calculate statistics
years_2010_2020 = [str(year) for year in range(2010, 2021)]
articles_per_year = {}

# Initialize
for year in years_2010_2020:
    articles_per_year[year] = 0

# Count articles per year
for article in europe_metadata:
    year = article['publication_date'][:4]
    if year in years_2010_2020:
        articles_per_year[year] += 1

# Calculate average
total_articles = sum(articles_per_year.values())
num_years = len(articles_per_year)
average_per_year = total_articles / num_years

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'articles_per_year': articles_per_year,
    'total_articles': total_articles,
    'num_years': num_years,
    'average_per_year': round(average_per_year, 2)
}))

var_functions.query_db:5.title: Carlyle Looks Toward Commercial Aerospace (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:5.description: Reuters - Private investment firm Carlyle Group,\which has a reputation for making well-timed and occasionally\controversial plays in the defense industry, has quietly placed\its bets on another part of the market.

var_functions.query_db:5.title: Oil and Economy Cloud Stocks' Outlook (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:5.description: Reuters - Soaring crude prices plus worries\about the economy and the outlook for earnings are expected to\hang over the stock market next week during the depth of the\summer doldrums.

var_functions.query_db:5.title: Iraq Halts Oil Exports from Main Southern Pipeline (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:5.description: Reuters - Authorities have halted oil export\flows from the main pipeline in southern Iraq after\intelligence showed a rebel militia could strike\infrastructure, an oil official said on Saturday.

var_functions.query_db:5.description: AFP - Tearaway world oil prices, toppling records and straining wallets, present a new economic menace barely three months before the US presidential elections.

var_functions.query_db:16.title: Google IPO Auction Off to Rocky Start
var_functions.query_db:16.description: WASHINGTON/NEW YORK (Reuters) - The auction for Google  Inc.'s highly anticipated initial public offering got off to a  rocky start on Friday after the Web search company sidestepped  a bullet from U.S. securities regulators.

var_functions.query_db:16.title: Dollar Falls Broadly on Record Trade Gap
var_functions.query_db:16.description: NEW YORK (Reuters) - The dollar tumbled broadly on Friday  after data showing a record U.S. trade deficit in June cast  fresh doubts on the economy's recovery and its ability to draw  foreign capital to fund the growing gap.
